summary: Legacy validated record without machine-checkable validation proof. Officially documented user environment-variable bundle for opting PowerShell and the .NET CLI out of telemetry. Good for privacy-focused shell workflows, but it can reduce telemetry available for product improvement and support diagnostics.

setting.name: PowerShell and .NET CLI Telemetry Opt-Out
setting.casual_explanation: PowerShell and the .NET CLI can send telemetry about tool usage. This tweak sets the current user's opt-out environment variables so new shells and CLI sessions stop sending that telemetry.
setting.why_people_change_it: People change it when they want quieter developer tooling, less usage reporting from command-line tools, or a privacy-focused shell profile.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Opting out reduces telemetry from PowerShell and the .NET CLI, but some Microsoft diagnostic signals and product-improvement feedback from those tools are lost, and new shell sessions may be needed before the change is visible everywhere.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents POWERSHELL_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT and DOTNET_CLI_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT as the supported opt-out controls. Microsoft also documents that persistent user environment variables live under HKEY_USERS\<USER_SID>\Environment, which corresponds to HKCU\Environment for the current user. PowerShell documents that true, yes, and 1 opt out. The .NET CLI documents true and 1 for opt-out. The current app writes 1 for both variables.

allowed_values.label: Default telemetry behavior
allowed_values.meaning: PowerShell uses its normal telemetry behavior because no persistent opt-out variable is set for the current user.

allowed_values.label: Telemetry opted out
allowed_values.meaning: PowerShell telemetry is opted out for the current user.

allowed_values.label: Telemetry opted out
allowed_values.meaning: PowerShell telemetry is opted out for the current user.

allowed_values.label: Telemetry opted out
allowed_values.meaning: PowerShell telemetry is opted out for the current user.

targets.notes: HKCU\Environment is the current-user view of the persistent user environment-variable store.

allowed_values.label: Default telemetry behavior
allowed_values.meaning: The .NET CLI uses its normal telemetry behavior because no persistent opt-out variable is set for the current user.

allowed_values.label: .NET CLI telemetry opted out
allowed_values.meaning: The .NET CLI telemetry is opted out for the current user.

allowed_values.label: .NET CLI telemetry opted out
allowed_values.meaning: The .NET CLI telemetry is opted out for the current user.

targets.notes: HKCU\Environment persists the current user's environment variables across new sessions.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes both documented opt-out variables as REG_SZ value 1 under HKCU\Environment.

windows_defaults.label: Windows default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Current-user profiles that have not explicitly set CLI telemetry opt-out variables

states.rationale: Leave the PowerShell opt-out variable unset.
Leave the .NET CLI opt-out variable unset.

windows_defaults.notes: Without these user environment variables, PowerShell and the .NET CLI keep their normal telemetry baseline.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Developer machines that want the default Microsoft telemetry baseline
avoid_for: Managed developer workstations that should opt out

states.rationale: Leave the PowerShell opt-out variable unset.
Leave the .NET CLI opt-out variable unset.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want PowerShell and the .NET CLI to keep their default telemetry behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want to minimize usage reporting from developer command-line tools.
tradeoffs: PowerShell and the .NET CLI keep their normal telemetry baseline.
No user-level privacy opt-out is enforced for these tools.

recommended_profiles.label: CLI privacy
intended_for: Users who want both tools opted out
avoid_for: Users who want to leave Microsoft CLI telemetry enabled

states.rationale: Set the documented PowerShell opt-out variable.
Set the documented .NET CLI opt-out variable.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want PowerShell and the .NET CLI to stop sending their default telemetry for the current user.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want to leave these tools on their default telemetry baseline for diagnostics or product feedback.
tradeoffs: PowerShell and .NET CLI telemetry are opted out for the current user.
New terminal windows or new shell sessions may be needed before all tools pick up the updated environment.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: about_Environment_Variables
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents POWERSHELL_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT and says values such as true, yes, and 1 opt PowerShell out of telemetry.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: Telemetry for the .NET CLI
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents DOTNET_CLI_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT and says setting it to 1 or true opts the .NET CLI out of telemetry.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: Win32_Environment class
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents that persistent user environment variables are stored under HKEY_USERS\<USER_SID>\Environment, which corresponds to HKCU\Environment for the current user.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes POWERSHELL_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT = 1 and DOTNET_CLI_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT = 1 under HKCU\Environment.

decision.why: Microsoft directly documents POWERSHELL_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT and DOTNET_CLI_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT as supported opt-out environment variables, and documents that persistent user environment variables are stored under HKEY_USERS\<user>\Environment, which corresponds to HKCU\Environment for the current user. The app writes the documented opt-out value 1 for both variables on that exact user environment surface.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: PowerShell: `POWERSHELL_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT` and `To opt-out of telemetry, set the environment variable to true, yes, or 1.`; .NET CLI telemetry: `set the DOTNET_CLI_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT environment variable to 1 or true`; Win32_Environment: `HKEY_USERS\<user>\Environment`.
validation_proof.notes: Microsoft documents both CLI telemetry opt-out variables and the accepted opt-out values. Microsoft also documents that persistent user environment variables live under HKEY_USERS\<user>\Environment, which corresponds to HKCU\Environment for the current user. The app writes POWERSHELL_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT = 1 and DOTNET_CLI_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT = 1 on that exact documented user environment surface.
